finding: Sessions ending past 23:00 local time observed on Apr 7 (5 sessions active past 23:00), Apr 10 (5 sessions), Apr 11 (8 sessions), and Apr 14 (1 session extending to 23:15). The late-night work on these days was interactive (not automated), covering hooks development, store listing text, config tweaks, and project work. A late-night warning hook already exists (late-night-warn.py, triggers after 20:00 with 5+ hours active) but the pattern persists, suggesting the nudge may not be sufficient or is being dismissed. The user's stated wellbeing goals include mindful breaks and not marathoning.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py hook exists and fires after 20:00 with 5+ active hours. It appears to not prevent the pattern.

finding: April 11 stands out as an extreme marathon day: 36 sessions from 08:18 to 23:37 local time, spanning jhana, weekend, and .claude projects. Scope expanded progressively throughout the day -- starting with jhana compilation work, branching into weekend repo exploration, MCP server testing, Android emulator work, voice loop verification, terminal config, and scheduling skill creation -- all in a single day. This matches the manic mode signature: multiple sessions within hours, scope escalating mid-session, short intense bursts. The user's north star explicitly includes being 'present in my body' and 'mindful breaks from screen.' April 12 followed with another 34-session day (08:38-19:35), though it ended earlier.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py fires a nudge but does not address daytime marathon patterns or scope expansion.

finding: From April 10 through April 14, five consecutive days showed elevated session counts: 11, 36, 34, 14, and 31 sessions respectively. No rest day or low-activity day appears in this window. The pattern shows a sustained high-intensity streak with no visible recovery period. Apr 10 started late (21:46) and ran to 23:28; Apr 11-12 were full-day marathons; Apr 13 was somewhat lighter but still 14 sessions from 08:10 to 17:00; Apr 14 ramped back up to 31 sessions from 08:40 to 23:15. This five-day sustained intensity with no breaks between days is a burnout precursor. The user's wellbeing goals mention mixing productive output with being 'very human: present, mindful, high wellbeing.'
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py addresses only late-night usage, not multi-day sustained intensity.

finding: On April 14, after a full day of work starting at 08:40, sessions continued past midnight into April 15 (last manual session at 00:29 local time). The late work included whisper-writer optimization, jonathans-assistant, and recursive self-improvement variance test launches. This is notable as the tail end of the five-day intensity streak (Apr 10-14), suggesting difficulty disengaging even at the end of a long run. However, some of the post-midnight activity was automated (queue-operation at 02:10, 04:19, 07:10), so the actual human involvement ended around 00:30.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py would have triggered around 20:00 but user continued for 4+ more hours.
